event_text: The Song dynasty and Liao dynasty agree to the Chanyuan Treaty.
The Western Xia state is proclaimed in northwest China.
The Wujing Zongyao records early gunpowder formulas and military technology.
Wang Anshi begins major reforms under the Song dynasty.
The Jingkang Incident ends the Northern Song and begins the Southern Song.
The Treaty of Shaoxing defines relations between Southern Song and Jin.
Temujin is proclaimed Genghis Khan, founding the Mongol Empire.
The Mongols destroy the Jin dynasty.
Kublai Khan proclaims the Yuan dynasty.
The Yuan defeat the Southern Song at Yamen, completing conquest of China.
Kublai Khan dies after establishing Mongol rule over China.
The Red Turban Rebellion begins against Yuan rule.
Zhu Yuanzhang founds the Ming dynasty and drives Yuan rulers from Dadu.
Zheng He’s first voyage leaves China, beginning seven major maritime expeditions.
The Ming capital moves to Beijing.
The Tumu Crisis weakens Ming military prestige.
Altan Khan raids near Beijing during Ming-Mongol conflict.
Portugal establishes a permanent settlement at Macau.
Matteo Ricci begins mission work in China.
Japanese forces invade Korea, leading Ming China to intervene during the Imjin War.
Matteo Ricci enters Beijing and builds influence at court.
Nurhaci proclaims the Later Jin state.
The Ming dynasty falls and the Qing dynasty takes Beijing.
Koxinga captures Taiwan from the Dutch.
Qing forces take Taiwan from the Zheng kingdom.
The Treaty of Nerchinsk defines a border with Russia.
Qing forces enter Tibet and establish greater influence there.
The Canton System restricts European trade to Guangzhou.
The Macartney Embassy seeks expanded British trade with China.
Armed conflict near Kowloon marks the opening phase of the First Opium War.
The Treaty of Nanking cedes Hong Kong to Britain and opens treaty ports.
Hong Xiuquan proclaims the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom, beginning the Taiping Rebellion.
The Arrow incident triggers the crisis that leads into the Second Opium War.
The Self-Strengthening Movement attempts military and industrial reform.
China is defeated by Japan in the First Sino-Japanese War.
The Hundred Days Reform attempts rapid institutional reform.
Boxer attacks on Christians in Shandong mark the opening phase of the Boxer Rebellion.
The Xinhai Revolution begins and leads to the fall of the Qing.
The Republic of China is established.
Yuan Shikai dies and the Warlord Era intensifies.
The May Fourth Movement protests foreign concessions and sparks cultural change.
The Chinese Communist Party is founded in Shanghai.
The Northern Expedition expands Nationalist control.
Japan occupies Manchuria after the Mukden Incident.
The Long March becomes a defining event for the Communist movement.
The Second Sino-Japanese War devastates China.
The Nanjing Massacre occurs after Japanese forces capture the city.
Japan surrenders and Taiwan is placed under Chinese administration.
The Chinese Civil War resumes between Nationalists and Communists.
Mao Zedong proclaims the People’s Republic of China.
China enters the Korean War.
The Great Leap Forward causes economic disruption and famine.
China tests its first atomic bomb.
The Cultural Revolution causes major political and social upheaval.
The People’s Republic of China takes China’s seat at the United Nations.
US President Richard Nixon visits China.
Mao Zedong dies and the Tangshan earthquake kills hundreds of thousands.
Deng Xiaoping launches reform and opening policies.
Protests in Tiananmen Square are violently suppressed.
Hong Kong is transferred from Britain to China.
Macau is transferred from Portugal to China.
China joins the World Trade Organization.
Beijing hosts the Olympic Games.
Expo 2010 opens in Shanghai.
The Belt and Road Initiative is announced.
Large protests take place in Hong Kong.
The COVID-19 outbreak leads to major public health responses in China and worldwide.
Beijing hosts the Winter Olympic Games.
